methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $200M - $3.5B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: QuantumScape
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

data.narrative:
LanzaTech is a public-stage climate and energy company founded by Sean Simpson and Richard Forster, currently valued at $800M+ after raising over $500M. The valuation assessment draws from a peer set of 10 same-sector, similar-valuation-tier comparables spanning both private (8) and public (1) companies, including Form Energy ($3.5B), Sila Nanotechnologies ($3.3B), KoBold Metals ($2.0B), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B), and Amogy ($700M).

The peer-median approach anchors the analysis at $600M, derived from the median valuation across the 10-company climate and energy peer set. The broader comparable universe ranges from $200M at the low end to $3.5B at the high end, reflecting significant dispersion typical of early-stage frontier technology. This single triangulation method yielded an implied valuation of $600M.

Synthesizing the available methodology supports a valuation range of $510M to $690M, with a central estimate of $600M. The current $800M+ valuation sits above this range, suggesting either a premium relative to peer comparables or valuation support from metrics not captured in this peer analysis. Key uncertainties include the limited applicability of comparables in a sector where asset bases, revenue maturity, and capital intensity vary substantially across the peer set. Additional revenue, margin, or technology-specific metrics would be required to refine this assessment.
